description: Advances: specled.spec_review — critical-review F2 (Medium): the scaffolded workflow builds untrusted PR code while holding contents:write/pull-requests:write in the same job that pushes gh-pages. Split render (read-only, untrusted code) from deploy/comment (write token, no PR code execution) via artifact handoff between jobs. Evidence: vault code_review/critical/specled_ex/2026-07-22/specled_-123-bootstrap-gaps/20260722-203631-security.md M1.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: specled_-3q1-harden-spec-review-yml (implement)
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/

Commit: f63f14b
Files touched:
  - priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex
  - .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md
Tests added: none — requirement is workflow_file-verified (no command tier)
Verification stubs flipped: none
Verification tier: change-scoped — mix compile --warnings-as-errors (clean),
  mix spec.check --base main (pass, exit 0), mix format --check-formatted,
  mix deps.unlock --check-unused, YAML parse + manual GitHub Actions schema
  review (two jobs, needs, per-job permissions, matching artifact name,
  download-path→cp-path alignment, @v4 action refs). full-system proof
  deferred to the merge gate / PR CI.
Final-tier runs: 1
Deferred must requirements: none

Summary: Split the scaffolded spec-review workflow into a read-only `render`
job that executes untrusted PR code (mix spec.review) and a write-scoped
`deploy` job that only handles the rendered HTML artifact (gh-pages push + PR
comment), with an artifact handoff between them and read-only top-level
permissions. No single job now both runs PR-provided code and holds a
write-scoped token. Added requirement
specled.spec_review.gh_pages_privilege_separation capturing this invariant,
covered by the existing workflow_file verification. The template is also
surfaced by specled.package; a Spec-Drift: branch_guard_missing_subject_update=info
trailer records that the hardening does not change the package's packaging
contract.

VERIFY: FAIL
Session: 53ad39e3-f64a-464f-9d21-327ebfcb00b4 (verify, standalone cold-context)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/53ad39e3-f64a-464f-9d21-327ebfcb00b4.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched (git diff main..HEAD --name-only): .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex — exactly the two expected; both in the specled.spec_review surface list. No unrelated files. PASS.
- cover_id strings in eex: both `# covers: specled.spec_review.gh_pages_distribution` (line 1) and `# covers: specled.spec_review.gh_pages_privilege_separation` (line 2) present. workflow_file verification target present. PASS.
- Spec-Drift trailer present in commit body: `Spec-Drift: branch_guard_missing_subject_update=info`. PASS.
- mix spec.check --base main: status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, findings=1 (branch_guard, downgraded to info), EXIT 0. PASS.
- YAML parse (python3 yaml.safe_load): parses clean, no EEx tags in template. PASS.

Semantic checks (corpus-scoped, doc/workflow-shaped diff — whole-file YAML validated by execution + structural review):
- Privilege split invariant #1 (no single job runs PR code + holds write token): render job runs `mix spec.review` (untrusted PR head) with `permissions: contents: read` only; deploy job holds `contents: write` + `pull-requests: write`, `needs: render`, runs NO mix/build steps (only git/gh/cp of the artifact). HOLDS.
- Top-level `permissions: contents: read` (read-only default). HOLDS.
- Artifact handoff coherent: upload name `spec-review-html` == download name `spec-review-html`; upload path `spec_review.html`, download `path: rendered`, deploy `cp rendered/spec_review.html ...` — source path lines up, file exists where copied from. HOLDS.
- Corpus-scoped claim validation: the SECURITY claim only partially holds. The requirement statement (authored in this same commit) says the write-scoped job "shall not check OUT or execute pull-request-provided code." The deploy job's `actions/checkout@v4` (step "Checkout repository") specifies no `ref`. On a `pull_request` event that defaults to the PR merge ref (refs/pull/<n>/merge), which IS pull-request-provided code — so the write-scoped job DOES check out PR code. It never executes it (no mix/build; only git/gh/cp), so the "execute" clause and the real F2/M1 vuln (executing untrusted code under a write token) are fixed — but the "check out" clause of the MUST requirement is not satisfied. This is a spec-vs-YAML overclaim.

Verdict: FAIL

Gaps:
1. gh_pages_privilege_separation (MUST) — the write-scoped deploy job checks out pull-request-provided code, which the requirement explicitly forbids ("shall not check out or execute"). deploy's `actions/checkout@v4` has no `ref`/`persist-credentials: false`; on `pull_request` the default checkout is the PR merge commit. Regression test / failure scenario: a malicious PR's tree lands on disk in the job holding contents:write + pull-requests:write. No execution path today, so practical risk is low, but the requirement statement is literally unmet and the spec overclaims what the YAML does. Fix is small: either pin the deploy checkout to a trusted ref (e.g. `ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}` / default branch) so no PR code is checked out, or narrow the requirement wording from "check out or execute" to "execute" — one of spec or YAML must move to eliminate the mismatch.

Notes:
- Core security value IS delivered: the exact critical-review F2/M1 finding (untrusted PR code executing while holding write scopes) is resolved — no job both runs PR code and holds a write token.
- No new external deps; action refs pinned @v4/@v1; no existing requirement weakened; gh_pages_distribution (should) still satisfied (mix spec.review on open/synchronize/reopened, per-PR gh-pages deploy, PR comment upsert).
- Did not close the ticket; no fixes applied; report only.

IMPLEMENTED (iteration 2 — verifier bounce fix)
Session: specled_-3q1-harden-spec-review-yml (implement)

Commit: cda8272 (amended)
Bounce addressed: VERIFY: FAIL on specled.spec_review.gh_pages_privilege_separation —
  the deploy (write-scoped) job's actions/checkout had no ref and defaulted to the
  PR merge commit, checking out PR-provided code, which the requirement forbids.
Fix: pinned the deploy checkout to the trusted base branch
  (`ref: ${{ github.base_ref }}`), so no pull-request-provided code enters the
  write-scoped job — only the base ref and the downloaded render artifact.
Files touched (unchanged set):
  - priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex
  - .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md
Verification tier: change-scoped — YAML parse OK, mix spec.check --base main (exit 0),
  mix format --check-formatted (exit 0). full-system proof deferred to merge gate / PR CI.
Final-tier runs: 2

Summary: Deploy job now checks out only the trusted base branch, closing the
"shall not check out ... pull-request-provided code" clause; the render/deploy
privilege split and artifact handoff are otherwise unchanged.

VERIFY: PASS (re-verify of amended HEAD cda8272)
Session: 53ad39e3-f64a-464f-9d21-327ebfcb00b4 (verify, standalone cold-context)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/53ad39e3-f64a-464f-9d21-327ebfcb00b4.jsonl

Supersedes my prior FAIL comment. The single Gap is resolved.

Delta since prior review: the ONLY change vs the FAIL'd commit is the deploy job's checkout — step renamed "Checkout base branch" and now pinned `with: ref: ${{ github.base_ref }}` (plus an explanatory comment). Render job, artifact handoff, per-job/top-level permissions, and the spec addition are byte-identical to my prior PASS-except-one-gap assessment.

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched (git diff main..HEAD --name-only): still exactly .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md + priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex; both in specled.spec_review surface; no unrelated files. PASS.
- cover ids: both gh_pages_distribution (line 1) and gh_pages_privilege_separation (line 2) present in eex; workflow_file verification target present. Spec requirement + covers entry unchanged. PASS.
- Spec-Drift trailer present: `branch_guard_missing_subject_update=info`. PASS.
- mix spec.check --base main: status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, findings=1 (branch_guard, info), EXIT 0. PASS.
- YAML parse (python3 yaml.safe_load): parses clean. PASS.

Semantic checks (corpus-scoped, whole-file YAML validated by execution + structural review):
- Privilege split invariant #1 (no single job runs PR code + holds write token): render runs `mix spec.review` (untrusted PR head) with contents:read only; deploy holds contents:write + pull-requests:write, needs: render, and executes NO build/mix command (verified per-step: only actions/checkout, actions/download-artifact, and git/gh/cp — the "mix" substring in deploy appears solely inside the PR-comment body text, not as an invocation). HOLDS.
- "shall not CHECK OUT ... pull-request-provided code" clause (the prior gap): now HOLDS. deploy checkout is pinned to `${{ github.base_ref }}`. On a pull_request event github.base_ref is the target/base branch NAME (e.g. "main") — repo-owner-controlled, resolves to the base branch tip, never the PR head or the refs/pull/<n>/merge ref. No PR-provided code is checked out into the write-scoped job.
- Top-level permissions contents:read (read-only default). HOLDS.
- Artifact handoff coherent: upload name spec-review-html == download name spec-review-html; upload path spec_review.html, download path: rendered, deploy `cp rendered/spec_review.html ...` — aligned. HOLDS.
- No regression to gh-pages push: actions/checkout@v4 with ref:<branch> keeps persist-credentials default (true), so the deploy job still has an authenticated origin remote + token; `git push origin gh-pages` path is intact. Base-branch checkout provides a full repo. HOLDS.
- Corpus-scoped claim validation: the spec statement now accurately describes the YAML with no overclaim — both the "check out" and "execute" clauses of gh_pages_privilege_separation are satisfied.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- gh_pages_privilege_separation (MUST): fully satisfied. Regression guard — the workflow_file verification pins the invariant; a future edit reintroducing an unpinned/PR-head checkout in the write-scoped job, moving write scopes into render, or adding a build/mix step to deploy would violate the requirement statement.
- gh_pages_distribution (should): still satisfied (mix spec.review on opened/synchronize/reopened, per-PR gh-pages deploy, PR-comment upsert).
- No new external deps; action refs pinned @v4/@v1; no existing requirement weakened; critical-review F2/M1 resolved.
- Ticket not closed; no fixes applied by verifier; report only.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: 53ad39e3-f64a-464f-9d21-327ebfcb00b4 (audit, standalone cold-context)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/53ad39e3-f64a-464f-9d21-327ebfcb00b4.jsonl

Log:
- bw show specled_-3q1 (read ticket + all 4 comments: IMPLEMENTED, VERIFY FAIL, IMPLEMENTED iter-2, VERIFY PASS).
- Read .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md (gh_pages_distribution should, gh_pages_privilege_separation must, workflow_file verification block).
- Read full priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex at HEAD (whole file, not just diff) + git diff main..HEAD.
- Read .spec/specs/package.spec.md to judge the Spec-Drift trailer.
- git diff main..HEAD --name-only; git log -1 --format=%B (trailer confirmed in HEAD cda8272).
- Did not execute mix test / mix spec.check; relied on recorded spec.check exit-0 in both VERIFY comments per audit posture (traced YAML/spec by hand).

Reasoning (traced every must clause of gh_pages_privilege_separation against the YAML):
1. Job split: render (L54) vs deploy (L105). render runs `mix spec.review` against PR head (L91-95) — the only untrusted-code execution. deploy has needs: render (L106). CONFIRMED.
2. Per-job scopes: render `permissions: contents: read` (L58-59); deploy `contents: write` + `pull-requests: write` (L110-112) — exactly the pair named in the statement. CONFIRMED.
3. Artifact handoff: upload name `spec-review-html` path `spec_review.html` if-no-files-found:error (L97-103); download name `spec-review-html` path `rendered` (L123-127); deploy `cp rendered/spec_review.html ...` (L151). Names match, path→cp source aligns, single-file upload glob (no traversal). CONFIRMED.
4. deploy runs no PR code: per-step audit of deploy — actions/checkout, actions/download-artifact, and git/gh/cp only. The one `mix` substring (L177) is inside the PR-comment printf body text, not an invocation. CONFIRMED. Verifier's iter-1 gap (unpinned deploy checkout defaulting to PR merge ref) is resolved: deploy checkout is pinned `ref: ${{ github.base_ref }}` (L119-121). On a pull_request event github.base_ref is the base BRANCH NAME in the base repo (repo-owner-controlled, e.g. "main") — never the PR head or refs/pull/N/merge; a fork attacker cannot create branches in the base repo, so it always resolves to trusted base-repo code. "shall not check out or execute PR code" now holds on both clauses.
5. Top-level `permissions: contents: read` (L46-47) — read-only default. CONFIRMED.
6. No overclaim: the requirement statement (authored this commit) accurately describes the YAML on every clause.

Residual-attack-surface sweep (checked the paths the task named, to test soundness of the PASS):
- Trigger is `pull_request` (L42), NOT pull_request_target — the safe choice; fork PRs get a read-only token regardless of the permissions block.
- Cache poisoning: actions/cache is in render only (L72-80); deploy restores no cache, so a render-poisoned cache cannot reach the write-scoped job. render is read-only anyway.
- persist-credentials: render's checkout persists a token PR code could read from .git/config, but it is the read-only token (render scope) — no write escalation. This is exactly why the split is sound.
- Script injection: deploy shell interpolates only github.sha (a hash) and github.repository / PR number (integer) — no attacker-controlled strings. Artifact name is static.
- Artifact content is attacker-influenced HTML but only copied and published to a per-PR gh-pages path, never executed — the design explicitly accepts this ("can influence rendered HTML, cannot push/comment as the token").
- Fork-PR note (not a defect, not in scope): on pull_request from a fork the deploy push would 403 (read-only token) — but the PRE-split single-job workflow had the identical fork limitation, so no regression, and this is the safe behavior, not a weakening of gh_pages_distribution.

Mechanical scope:
- git diff main..HEAD --name-only = exactly .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md + priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex. The eex is in the specled.spec_review surface (spec_review.spec.md L26); the spec file is the subject's own definition. No unrelated files. PASS.
- specled.package co-change: the eex is ALSO in the package surface (package.spec.md L36), so its change trips branch_guard for package. Handled by `Spec-Drift: branch_guard_missing_subject_update=info` (confirmed in HEAD body; spec.check recorded findings=1 downgraded to info, exit 0). Trailer is HONEST: no package requirement governs the workflow's internal job structure (mix_tasks/index/governance/adoption/concepts/doc_identifier all unchanged; the eex carries no finding-code references, so doc_identifier_integrity is untouched). The package still ships the same scaffold/tasks/docs — packaging contract genuinely unchanged; info is the correct classification.
- gh_pages_distribution (should) not weakened: still on [opened, synchronize, reopened] (L43), still deploys to pr/<N>/ on gh-pages, still upserts the marker-anchored PR comment (L166-191).
- No mix deps added (mix.lock untouched); the two new refs are standard GitHub actions/upload-artifact@v4 + actions/download-artifact@v4, pinned. No unrelated behavior change.

Verdict: APPROVE. Every clause of the gh_pages_privilege_separation MUST is genuinely satisfied by the YAML with no overclaim; the F2/M1 critical-review finding (untrusted code executing under a write token) is resolved; the diff respects scope and the package trailer is honest. The verifier's PASS was sound — the residual-path sweep (pull_request_target, cache, persist-credentials, injection, artifact) surfaces nothing that reaches a write token. Not closing the ticket; no fixes applied.

APPROVED per cold verifier (VERIFY: PASS) + cold auditor (AUDIT: APPROVE) sign-off. Non-worktree status: implemented on branch specled_-3q1/split-render-deploy (HEAD cda8272), NOT yet merged to main — land via PR (/open-pr). Change-scoped gates green (compile, spec.check, format, deps.unlock); full mix test deferred to PR CI per skill. Closing per auditor sign-off.

Shipped as 0.3.3 — squash-merged to main (8f7abd3, PR #8). Post-merge Specs green. Rebased over #9 (which took 0.3.2). Follow-up specled_-vbo tracks the command-tier verification (critical-review F1).

title: Harden spec_review.yml.eex: split untrusted-PR build from write-scoped deploy job
